title: Lenin quote — 'I agree to everything in advance'
summary: From a letter to Bogdanov & Gusev (Feb 1905): Lenin signs off in exasperation before a party congress.
body:
Letter to A. A. Bogdanov and S. I. Gusev, 11 February 1905.

'I reply in advance that I agree to everything. Good-bye until the Congress.'

Context: Lenin, fed up with endless procedural back-and-forth over party organisation, signs off with characteristic irony before the forthcoming congress. Classic Lenin — sardonic, impatient, and cutting.
